caption.text: In-app notifications are one of the most powerful levers to
activate and retain
your users.
And yet, they are often misused.
So today, I'm giving you 5 mistakes I see everywhere.
The 1st is notifying to remind people you exist.
"You haven't logged in for 3 days",
"we miss you".
These notifications provide no value
so they give no reason to return.
They annoy and often end up getting disabled.
So 1 notification must always answer 1 question:
what does my user gain by clicking on it?
The 2nd is notifying too often. With every new feature, updates,
tips. Your user is overwhelmed, so they start ignoring all
notifications,
even the most important ones. Because you've exhausted their attention,
so the less you notify, the more each
notification counts.
The 3rd is notifying without any possible action. Your user receives 1
notification, clicks on it, and then nothing, just information,
a number or a stat, but no action to take. So they close it and leave. 1
notification without action is 1 wasted opportunity.
Ideally, it should lead somewhere. A candidate just applied,
view their profile. Your report is ready,
check it. Information + action.
The 4th is notifying them about something that doesn't directly concern them.
"Discover our new blog",
"see what others are doing". They don't care, 1
in-app notification should be triggered by something that concerns them,
and not necessarily by something you want to tell them.
For example, a candidate applying,
a payment received, or a task completed by their team.
That is a notification worthy of their attention.
And the 5th is not giving control to the user.
If they can't choose which
notification they receive, they will disable them all.
Give them control and let them choose what's important to them.
It's counterintuitive, but trust me, it increases engagement.
In fact, the ideal notification is the one your user was waiting for
without knowing they were waiting for it.
